title: IQM Quantum Computers Acquires Quantistry Assets to Form Full-Stack Industrial Simulation Platform - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Superconducting quantum hardware maker IQM (Nasdaq: IQMX) acquired the software, IP, and chemical-simulation algorithms of Berlin-based Quantistry GmbH to build a full-stack, software-plus-hardware industrial simulation platform targeting automotive, aerospace, chemical, and pharma R&D.

insights.claim: Superconducting quantum hardware developer IQM Quantum Computers (Nasdaq: IQMX) completed an asset acquisition of Berlin-based Quantistry GmbH, absorbing its proprietary software, algorithm libraries, and intellectual property.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals hardware-first quantum vendors buying software/algorithm IP to move up the value stack; watch IQMX for further tuck-in acquisitions.
insights.quote: The transaction includes Quantistry’s proprietary software, algorithm libraries, and intellectual property.

insights.claim: IQM listed on Nasdaq via a business combination with Real Asset Acquisition Corp. (RAAQ), a SPAC vehicle, before executing the Quantistry asset purchase.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A freshly SPAC-listed quantum name deploying capital into M&A; scrutinize cash position and forward-looking financials in its press room disclosures.
insights.quote: The acquisition occurred following IQM’s business combination with Real Asset Acquisition Corp. (RAAQ), which listed the company on the Nasdaq Stock Market.
insights.locator: The acquisition occurred following IQM’s business combination

insights.claim: Quantistry's technical, quantum chemistry, and software engineering teams will join IQM to maintain development continuity as part of the asset deal.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Acqui-hire component suggests IQM is buying scarce quantum-chemistry software talent, not just code; retention risk is a key integration watch item.
insights.locator: Quantistry’s technical, quantum chemistry, and software engineering teams will join IQM

insights.claim: IQM intends to integrate Quantistry's software applications with its superconducting quantum hardware to build a single vertically integrated software-hardware development platform.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: IQM is betting on full-stack lock-in rather than selling raw qubits; a differentiator versus hardware-only quantum competitors.
insights.quote: IQM plans to integrate Quantistry’s software applications with its superconducting quantum hardware infrastructure to establish an integrated software-hardware development platform.
insights.locator: IQM plans to integrate Quantistry’s software applications

insights.claim: The combined platform is positioned to let industrial clients run classical simulations, machine learning configurations, and quantum hardware backends within one unified ecosystem, per IQM CEO Jan Goetz and Quantistry CEO Dr. Marcel Quennet.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Unified classical-plus-quantum ecosystems are the near-term commercial wedge for quantum firms while fault-tolerant hardware is still maturing.
insights.locator: According to IQM CEO Jan Goetz and Quantistry CEO Dr. Marcel Quennet

insights.claim: Quantistry's platform pairs high-performance computing with quantum chemical algorithms and uses a machine learning layer to automatically route workflows across classical HPC, AI computing environments, and quantum backends.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: ML-driven orchestration across classical, AI, and quantum compute is the emerging architecture for practical hybrid quantum workloads; a template competitors will copy.
insights.quote: An architectural feature of the platform is a machine learning layer that automates workflow routing across classical HPC infrastructures, AI computing environments, and quantum backends.
insights.locator: Quantistry’s software platform integrates high-performance computing

insights.claim: The software is designed to let industrial research teams model molecular geometries and physical properties without needing specialized quantum computing or chemical simulation expertise.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Abstracting away quantum/chemistry expertise widens the addressable enterprise user base, a key adoption lever for monetizing quantum simulation.
insights.quote: This software allows industrial research teams to model molecular geometries and physical properties without requiring specialized quantum computing or chemical simulation expertise.
insights.locator: This software allows industrial research teams

insights.claim: The integrated platform explicitly targets automotive, aerospace, chemical, materials science, and pharmaceutical R&D as its industrial end markets.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Defines IQM's commercial TAM; these R&D-heavy sectors are the near-term buyers of quantum-enhanced molecular simulation.
